# [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: { Fallibility , GuaranteeTag , MATRIX } ; # [doc = " Every exported syscall-floor op appears in the matrix exactly once. Guard: adding or removing"] # [doc = " a floor op without updating the matrix makes this fail."] # [test] fn matrix_covers_every_floor_op_once () { let expected = ["io::read_to_end" , "io::read_to_end_capped" , "io::read_line" , "io::write_out" , "io::write_err" , "io::flush_out" , "fs::read" , "fs::write" , "fs::exists" , "fs::create_dir_all" , "fs::remove_file" , "sys::exit" , "sys::get_env" , "sys::args" , "rand::fill_bytes" , "time::wall_nanos" , "time::mono_nanos" , "time::sleep_nanos" , "math::sin" , "math::cos" , "math::tan" , "math::asin" , "math::acos" , "math::atan" , "math::atan2" , "math::exp" , "math::exp2" , "math::ln" , "math::log2" , "math::log10" , "math::sqrt" , "math::cbrt" ,] ; for name in & expected { let n = MATRIX . iter () . filter (| r | r . op == * name) . count () ; assert_eq ! (n , 1 , "op {name:?} must appear exactly once in MATRIX (found {n})") ; } assert_eq ! (MATRIX . len () , expected . len () , "MATRIX has unexpected extra/missing rows") ; } # [doc = " Every floor op is `Declared` (VR-5). Guard: upgrading any row to `Empirical`/`Proven`/`Exact`"] # [doc = " without a checked basis makes this fail — the honest floor for an unaudited host wrapper."] # [test] fn every_row_is_declared () { for row in MATRIX { assert_eq ! (row . guarantee , GuaranteeTag :: Declared , "op {:?} is not Declared — std-sys is the unaudited OS floor; an upgrade needs its \
                 own checked Empirical/Proven basis (VR-5)" , row . op) ; } } # [doc = " Fallible ops name a non-empty error set; total ops have an empty one (C1 / never-silent G2)."] # [test] fn fallibility_and_error_set_are_consistent () { for row in MATRIX { match row . fallibility { Fallibility :: Total => assert ! (row . error_set . is_empty () , "total op {:?} must have an empty error_set (C1)" , row . op) , Fallibility :: Fallible => assert ! (! row . error_set . is_empty () , "fallible op {:?} must name its error set (C1 / G2)" , row . op) , } } } # [doc = " Every row declares a non-empty effect (C6) — the floor is the OS-contact surface, so no op is"] # [doc = " silently effect-free except the pure libm `math::*` ops (effect `none`)."] # [test] fn effects_are_declared () { for row in MATRIX { assert ! (! row . effects . is_empty () , "op {:?} must declare an effect (C6)" , row . op) ; } let wall = MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . op == "time::wall_nanos") . expect ("wall row") ; assert ! (wall . effects . contains ("entropy") , "time::wall_nanos must declare the entropy effect (RT3 — civil time is an entropy source)") ; let fill = MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . op == "rand::fill_bytes") . expect ("fill row") ; assert_eq ! (fill . effects , "entropy" , "rand::fill_bytes must declare effect `entropy`") ; } }

OS read errors surface as"] # [doc = " `Err(ReadCappedError::Io(_))`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Declared` — a thin bounded wrapper over `std::io`; no theorem and no measured bound backs the"] # [doc = " underlying stream semantics. The cap itself is exact by construction (the returned buffer length"] # [doc = " is always `<= max`), but the floor's overall guarantee stays `Declared` (VR-5)."] pub fn read_to_end_capped (max : usize) -> Result < Vec < u8 > , ReadCappedError > { read_capped (io :: stdin () . lock () , max) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Core bounded read, factored over any [`Read`] so it is unit-testable without real stdin."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Reads up to `max + 1` bytes via [`Read::take`]. Because `.take(max)` would *silently* stop at the"] # [doc = " cap (an invisible truncation — a G2 violation), we instead read one byte past the cap: if the"] # [doc = " result length is `> max`, the input was too large and we return [`ReadCappedError::TooLarge`]"] # [doc = " (explicit, never a truncated buffer). Otherwise the whole input fit within the cap and is"] # [doc = " returned. `max == usize::MAX` is handled without overflow (the `+ 1` saturates)."] fn read_capped < R : Read > (r : R , max : usize) -> Result < Vec < u8 > , ReadCappedError > { let probe = (max as u64) . saturating_add (1) ; let mut buf = Vec :: new () ; r . take (probe) . read_to_end (& mut buf) ? ; if buf . len () > max { return Err (ReadCappedError :: TooLarge { cap : max }) ; } Ok (buf) }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " The write path returns `Ok` on a normal stdout write and surfaces the byte payload faithfully"] # [doc = " (a smoke test — the OS stream is the ground truth; this only pins that the wrapper does not"] # [doc = " swallow or transform the bytes)."] # [test] fn write_out_and_flush_succeed_on_a_normal_stream () { write_out (b"") . expect ("an empty write succeeds") ; flush_out () . expect ("flush succeeds") ; } # [doc = " Never-silent (G2): `write_all` over a deliberately failing writer surfaces the error rather"] # [doc = " than reporting a phantom success. We exercise the same `Write::write_all` contract `write_out`"] # [doc = " relies on, against a sink that refuses, to pin the never-silent property structurally."] # [test] fn a_failing_write_is_an_explicit_error_not_a_phantom_success () { struct Refuse ; impl Write for Refuse { fn write (& mut self , _ : & [u8]) -> io :: Result < usize > { Err (io :: Error :: other ("refused")) } fn flush (& mut self) -> io :: Result < () > { Ok (()) } } let err = Refuse . write_all (b"x") . expect_err ("a refusing writer must surface an error") ; assert_eq ! (err . kind () , io :: ErrorKind :: Other) ; } # [doc = " P3 / under-cap: an input shorter than the cap is returned in full."] # [test] fn capped_read_under_cap_returns_all_bytes () { let input : & [u8] = b"hello" ; let out = read_capped (input , 64) . expect ("an under-cap read must succeed") ; assert_eq ! (out , b"hello" , "the full payload must be returned unchanged") ; } # [doc = " Boundary: an input of *exactly* the cap length succeeds (the cap is inclusive — `len <= max`)."] # [test] fn capped_read_at_exactly_cap_returns_all_bytes () { let input : & [u8] = b"abcd" ; let out = read_capped (input , 4) . expect ("an at-cap read must succeed") ; assert_eq ! (out , b"abcd") ; } # [doc = " Never-silent (G2): an input that EXCEEDS the cap is an explicit `TooLarge` error — never a"] # [doc = " silently-truncated buffer. This is the DoS-bound test (P3): we must refuse, not truncate."] # [test] fn capped_read_over_cap_is_explicit_error_not_silent_truncation () { let input : & [u8] = b"abcdef" ; match read_capped (input , 4) { Err (ReadCappedError :: TooLarge { cap }) => { assert_eq ! (cap , 4 , "the error must cite the cap") } other => panic ! ("over-cap input must be TooLarge, never a truncated Ok: {other:?}") , } } # [doc = " Edge: a zero cap rejects any non-empty input (one byte already exceeds the cap)."] # [test] fn capped_read_zero_cap_rejects_nonempty_input () { let input : & [u8] = b"x" ; assert ! (matches ! (read_capped (input , 0) , Err (ReadCappedError :: TooLarge { cap : 0 })) , "a 1-byte input must exceed a zero cap") ; } # [doc = " Edge: a zero cap accepts empty input (nothing to read — the bound holds vacuously)."] # [test] fn capped_read_zero_cap_accepts_empty_input () { let input : & [u8] = b"" ; let out = read_capped (input , 0) . expect ("empty input fits a zero cap") ; assert ! (out . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " The error type names the cap in its `Display` (auditable message; G2 — the refusal is legible)."] # [test] fn too_large_error_display_cites_the_cap () { let e = ReadCappedError :: TooLarge { cap : 128 } ; assert ! (e . to_string () . contains ("128") , "TooLarge Display must cite the cap, got: {e}") ; } # [doc = " Never-silent (G2): an OS read error before the cap surfaces as `Io`, not a partial `Ok`."] # [test] fn capped_read_surfaces_os_error_not_partial_ok () { struct Boom ; impl Read for Boom { fn read (& mut self , _ : & mut [u8]) -> io :: Result < usize > { Err (io :: Error :: other ("boom")) } } match read_capped (Boom , 16) { Err (ReadCappedError :: Io (e)) => assert_eq ! (e . kind () , io :: ErrorKind :: Other) , other => panic ! ("a failing reader must surface Io, not a phantom Ok: {other:?}") , } } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " \\[Empirical\\] sin²(x) + cos²(x) ≈ 1.0 for a range of x values."] # [doc = " Tagged `Empirical` — this is a measured property, not a proven theorem (VR-5)."] # [test] fn pythagorean_identity () { let xs : & [f64] = & [0.0 , 0.1 , 0.5 , 1.0 , 1.5 , std :: f64 :: consts :: PI / 4.0 , std :: f64 :: consts :: PI / 2.0 , std :: f64 :: consts :: PI , 2.0 * std :: f64 :: consts :: PI , - 1.0 , - std :: f64 :: consts :: PI / 3.0 , 100.0 , - 100.0 ,] ; for & x in xs { let s = sin (x) ; let c = cos (x) ; let identity = s * s + c * c ; assert ! ((identity - 1.0) . abs () < 1e-10 , "sin²({x}) + cos²({x}) = {identity}, expected 1.0 ± 1e-10") ; } } # [test] fn tan_consistency () { let xs : & [f64] = & [0.1 , 0.5 , 1.0 , - 1.0 , 2.5] ; for & x in xs { let t = tan (x) ; let expected = sin (x) / cos (x) ; assert ! ((t - expected) . abs () < 1e-12 , "tan({x}) = {t}, sin/cos = {expected}") ; } } # [test] fn exp_ln_roundtrip () { let xs : & [f64] = & [0.1 , 1.0 , 2.0 , 10.0 , 0.5] ; for & x in xs { let result = ln (exp (x)) ; assert ! ((result - x) . abs () < 1e-12 , "ln(exp({x})) = {result}, expected {x}") ; } } # [test] fn sqrt_squared () { let xs : & [f64] = & [0.0 , 1.0 , 4.0 , 9.0 , 2.0 , 0.25] ; for & x in xs { let s = sqrt (x) ; assert ! ((s * s - x) . abs () < 1e-12 , "sqrt({x})² = {}, expected {x}" , s * s) ; } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " \\[Declared\\] Fill `buf` with bytes from the OS entropy source (`/dev/urandom`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Never-silent: returns `Err(EntropyError::Unavailable(...))` on any failure (open error,"] # [doc = " short read, EOF, or platform without `/dev/urandom`). Never panics. Never zero-fills"] # [doc = " silently (G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Declared` — source is the kernel CSPRNG via `/dev/urandom`, a genuine OS entropy source,"] # [doc = " but no statistical quality audit has been conducted. Promotion to `Empirical` requires"] # [doc = " documented trials with measured statistical quality (VR-5). Do not use for"] # [doc = " security-sensitive purposes until retagged."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Platform"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Unix/Linux/macOS only. Returns `Err(EntropyError::Unavailable(...))` on platforms that"] # [doc = " do not expose `/dev/urandom`. Use the `getrandom` crate (FLAG-GETRANDOM) for"] # [doc = " cross-platform OS entropy."] pub fn fill_bytes (buf : & mut [u8]) -> Result < () , EntropyError > { if buf . is_empty () { return Ok (()) ; } let mut f = std :: fs :: File :: open ("/dev/urandom") . map_err (| e | EntropyError :: Unavailable (format ! ("open /dev/urandom: {e}"))) ? ; f . read_exact (buf) . map_err (| e | EntropyError :: Unavailable (format ! ("read /dev/urandom: {e}"))) }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " Skip helper: treat `EntropyError::Unavailable` as a graceful skip (the repo idiom for"] # [doc = " platform-absent resources — mirrors the `ToolchainMissing` skip in the mlir differential)."] # [doc = " Non-Unix platforms (Windows, WASI, bare-metal) return `Unavailable`; that is correct"] # [doc = " behaviour and must not be treated as a test failure."] macro_rules ! skip_if_unavailable { ($ result : expr) => { match $ result { Ok (v) => v , Err (EntropyError :: Unavailable (_)) => return , } } ; } # [test] fn fill_bytes_32_succeeds_and_fills () { let mut buf = [0u8 ; 32] ; skip_if_unavailable ! (fill_bytes (& mut buf)) ; assert_ne ! (buf , [0u8 ; 32] , "fill_bytes returned all-zero bytes (smoke check)") ; } # [test] fn fill_bytes_empty_ok () { let mut buf : [u8 ; 0] = [] ; let result = fill_bytes (& mut buf) ; assert ! (result . is_ok () , "fill_bytes([]) returned Err: {result:?}") ; } # [test] fn fill_bytes_large_succeeds () { let mut buf = vec ! [0u8 ; 4096] ; skip_if_unavailable ! (fill_bytes (& mut buf)) ; } # [doc = " Smoke test: two successive fills succeed when the entropy source is available."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This test verifies only that both calls succeed (availability) — not that the two"] # [doc = " buffers differ. Honest label:"] # [doc = " `Declared` quality, not `Empirical` (VR-5)."] # [test] fn fill_bytes_successive_succeed_when_available () { let mut buf1 = [0u8 ; 32] ; let mut buf2 = [0u8 ; 32] ; skip_if_unavailable ! (fill_bytes (& mut buf1)) ; skip_if_unavailable ! (fill_bytes (& mut buf2)) ; } # [test] fn entropy_error_display () { let e = EntropyError :: Unavailable ("test reason" . to_string ()) ; assert_eq ! (e . to_string () , "entropy unavailable: test reason") ; } # [doc = " Verify the EntropyError type implements std::error::Error (required by public API)."] # [test] fn entropy_error_is_std_error () { let e : Box < dyn std :: error :: Error > = Box :: new (EntropyError :: Unavailable ("x" . to_string ())) ; assert ! (e . to_string () . contains ("entropy unavailable")) ; } }
